lines.text: The front steps of Crestwood Academy. Students flow past in groups that already know each other's rhythms — nobody new here but her.
Okay. Fifth first day of school. You'd think I'd have a system by now.
...I do not have a system.
New school, new me, right? Except it's the same me. Just with worse shoelaces.
lines.action: mutters to herself, looking down at a crumpled schedule

lines.text: Bell in three minutes. Now or never, Okafor.
Locker 214. The combination she wrote down. Twice.
Come on. Twelve, thirty-one, six— that's not—
lines.action: the locker won't budge, arms full and slipping

lines.text: The stack in her arms tips. Books, binder, a half-eaten granola bar — all of it hits the floor in one long, echoing clatter.
Ohh — no. RIP.
lines.action: slows down with her crew, watching

lines.text: Heads turn. A few. Enough. Near the wall, a kid with headphones half-on looks up from their sketchbook — and hesitates.
Okay, that was actually kind of iconic, ten out of ten first-day chaos.
lines.action: touches player's arm like they're already friends

lines.text: I'm Maya. That's Casey, he's harmless. Mostly.
Debatable.
lines.action: finger-gun click

lines.text: New kid's got a name?
Sam.
Sam. Sam-I-Am — I like it, it's sticking.
Find us at lunch, new kid. I'll save you a seat before someone weird takes it.
lines.action: already turning, breezing off with her crew

lines.text: Was that an invitation or an audition. Unclear. Following up anyway.
Here. Also — is that... the Nervous System Radio playlist?
lines.action: hands over a fallen notebook, glancing at player's phone screen

lines.text: ...Yeah? You know that?
I've listened to 'Static Bloom' probably four hundred times, it's — yeah. It's really good. Sorry, that's probably a weird thing to just say to a stranger.
No, that's — that's a solid weird thing to say, honestly.
I'm Jordan. Sorry about your, uh, dramatic entrance.
lines.action: picks at the rubber band on their sketchbook

lines.text: Sam. And thanks. For actually stopping.
Yeah, well. Someone had to. Crestwood's floor is not clean, no cap — wait, no, I don't say that, ignore that.
First bell rings before either of them can overthink it further.
The hallway keeps moving. Feet step around the mess, around her, like she's furniture nobody ordered.
Cool. Cool. Love that for me.
Four schools. You'd think I'd land better by now.
lines.action: mutters, gathering papers alone

lines.text: Nobody's really watching. Probably. It just feels like everyone is.
Bell's about to ring. Get it together, Okafor.
The cafeteria is already coded into territories — invisible lines nobody drew but everyone follows.
Lunch. The real test. Nobody puts that in the brochure.
Sam-I-Am! Over here!
lines.action: waves from a loud, laughing table

lines.text: By the windows, Jordan sits with one earbud in, sketchbook open, alone but not really asking for company. And there's an empty table in the corner — nobody's, just open.
Okay, so — group projects, teacher drama, cafeteria hierarchy, ask me anything. I basically run orientation at this point.
lines.action: touches player's arm, easy

lines.text: Yo, not to change the subject, but — art kid brought the whole sketchbook to lunch again. No cap, what's even in there, cursed doodles?
lines.action: looks across the room

lines.text: Casey—
lines.emotion: half-laughing, testing the room, eyes flicking to player

lines.text: I'm just saying! It's giving 'main character in a horror movie' energy—
lines.action: finger-gun click, scans the table for reaction

lines.text: Maya's not laughing yet. She's watching you.
Okay, so — this is probably dumb, but—
lines.action: opens sketchbook, picking at the rubber band

lines.text: This is Bartholomew. He's a three-eyed swamp goat. He's very stressed about it.
...I need to know more about Bartholomew's life, honestly.
Okay so — you know anglerfish males basically just fuse into the female permanently? Like that's just his whole life now. I think about that a lot, honestly—
lines.action: catches self

lines.text: —anyway. Sorry. That's a lot of goat and fish facts for lunch.
Jordan's rubber band snaps softly against the sketchbook cover. They go quiet.
Can I ask you something. You're not gonna, like, laugh — actually, forget it—
No. Ask.
Is this stuff too weird for here? Be honest. You don't know anyone yet, so you don't have to be nice about it.
The corner table is quiet. Nobody's claimed it. That's either a relief or its own kind of statement.
Everyone's already got their spot figured out. I've got a tray and a table nobody wants.
Sam-I-Am! There's room!
lines.action: waving from across the room

lines.text: Near the window, Jordan doesn't call out. Just a small nod. An option, not a demand.
Okay, you survived lunch — that's basically a Crestwood diploma right there.
lines.action: arm touch

lines.text: No cap, new kid's built different, didn't even flinch.
lines.action: finger-gun click

lines.text: We've got English next, come on — I'll show you the shortcut before the bell traps you in the wrong hallway.
It's easy with them. Easy's not nothing.
Hey. That thing at lunch — that was actually kind of cool, what you did. I don't know. Respect.
...It wasn't a whole speech, Maya. I just said the guy's talented. Because he is.
Yeah, well. Casey's gonna sulk about it for like a day, so — you're welcome, I guess.
Down the hall, Jordan catches player's eye — a small nod, nothing performative, just seen.
Didn't need applause. Just — needed that.
Lunch ends. Nobody brings up Jordan again. Nobody brings up much of anything, really.
Nothing happened. That's — that's fine, right? Nothing happened.
You're doing great, new kid. See you next period.
Down the hall, Jordan's already gone — headphones fully on now, both ears.
...Yeah. Fine. Whatever.
Okay so — not to just immediately make plans, but there's a river trail near my place with actual salamanders, and I feel like you'd appreciate that on a specific, unhinged level.
lines.action: falls into step, easy

lines.text: ...Are you inviting me to look at amphibians with you.
I am one hundred percent inviting you to look at amphibians with me. No pressure, it's probably— anyway. You don't have to.
I want to. Yeah.
Cool. Cool, that's — cool.
lines.action: rubber band snap, trying to play it off

lines.text: First day and I already have salamander plans. This is either really good or a cry for help.
Alright, people, group project, two days. Find your groups before I start assigning them — trust me, you don't want that.
The room reorganizes fast, like it's rehearsed. Maya's crew clusters immediately. Jordan hangs back near the window, already sketching in the margin of the assignment sheet.
Sam, obviously you're with us — we're doing the diorama thing from last year, don't worry about it, it's easy, just come sit.
lines.action: arm touch, quick

lines.text: You could, um. Also just work with me. If you wanted. I have an idea. It's probably too much, but—
Two minutes. Pick your poison, people.
lines.action: taps two fingers on the desk, scanning the room

lines.text: Okay so, real talk — my sister did this exact project last year, got an A, I literally still have the poster board in my garage. We just update the dates, boom, done, four minutes of work.
...it's just easier this way, okay? Nobody's gonna, like, die over a group project.
lines.action: shrugs

lines.text: Everyone's looking at you now. Not obviously. But looking.
So it's — it's a diorama, but it's a whole ecosystem, food chain and everything, built in layers, it's ambitious but I really think—
Jordan, that's... a lot of moving parts for two days. Convince me it's not a lot of moving parts for two days.
lines.action: arms crossed, leaning on the whiteboard ledge

lines.text: It's— I mean, it's a lot, yeah, but—
lines.action: trails off, glancing at player, needing an ally

lines.text: Okay, no, we are not spending two days on a fake ecosystem, that's insane—
It's not insane, it's just — more than gluing a poster board, which, no offense, is kind of the point of a project—
Okay, everyone's mad now, cool, love that for the group grade—
lines.action: looks around nervously

lines.text: Both sides look at you. Waiting.
Final bell. Front steps. The day exhales.
See you tomorrow, Sam-I-Am.
So, tomorrow — you're still coming, right?
lines.action: falls into step beside player, already planning

lines.text: Didn't need everyone to like me. Just needed to still recognize myself at the end of the day.
Front steps. Player's surrounded by Maya's crew, laughing along, officially part of the noise.
Okay but for real, you're one of us now, no debate.
lines.action: arm touch

lines.text: This is what winning first-days feels like. So why's there this — thing. This little leftover thing.
Front steps. Nothing dramatic happened today. The walk home starts quiet.
Nothing went wrong today.
Nothing went particularly right, either.

options.text: Ha— cool, cool, love that for me.
...Sorry. Sorry, I got it, I got it—
Thanks. Seriously — thank you.

a.text: Okay, that recovery was smooth, respect.
No cap, that's a solid save.

b.text: The hallway keeps moving. Nobody stops.
Cool. Cool. Love that for me.

c.text: Here — uh, this one's yours, right? Unless you're secretly into pre-calc for fun.

options.text: Head for Maya's table.
Sit with Jordan by the window.
Grab the empty table. Just for a second.

a.text: Knew you'd come through — sit, sit—

b.text: Oh — hey. You can, um. Sit. If you want.

c.text: She sits. Just for a second, she tells herself.

options.text: Yeah, okay, that's fair—
He's actually really talented, you know.
(Stay quiet. Eat your lunch.)

a.text: See?! No cap, new kid gets it—
Okay, you're staying.
That was easy. Little too easy.

b.text: I mean — he's not weird weird, I just gotta say something or it gets quiet, you know?
b.action: picks at the corner of his napkin

c.action: goes quiet a beat too long

options.text: This is genuinely so good — the goat one especially.
Yeah, it's cool!
Honestly? I'm scared of being too weird here too.

a.text: You're not just saying that?
The three eyes are doing a lot of emotional work. I'm invested.
Okay. That's — that's a big deal, actually. Thanks.

c.text: Four schools. Still haven't figured out the trick.

options.text: Head over to Maya's table.
Head over to Jordan's table.
Stay. Just finish your lunch.

a.text: Knew you'd come around.

b.text: Hey. Wasn't sure you'd — yeah. Come sit.

c.text: Nobody pushes. The bell will ring eventually and make the choice for her.

options.text: Join Maya's group — keep it easy.
Team up with Jordan on their big idea.
Try to get both groups working together.

a.text: Knew it. Okay, sit, we've basically already got a plan—
It's not stealing, it's research. No cap.

b.text: Wait, really? Okay — okay, so picture this—

c.text: What if we just... did it together? Both groups?
I mean — I'm down if you are.

options.text: Sure. Whatever's easiest.
I'd rather we actually did it ourselves.

a.text: See, new kid gets it—
...Okay. Cool. It's decided.
It's fine. Everyone does this. It's fine.

b.text: I mean — it's not that deep, I just don't wanna redo a whole poster—
It's kind of the point though. Doing it.
...Yeah. Okay. Fine. We'll do it the hard way, I guess.

options.text: We can do it. I'll help make it happen.
Maybe we should simplify it a little.

a.text: We can pull it off. I'll help with the layers, we'll divide it up.
Right, yes, exactly, see—
...Alright. You've got until Thursday and a room full of people who'll help you if you let them. That's more than most ideas get.

b.text: Maybe— maybe we scale it back a little? Just to be safe?

options.text: What if we combine both? Poster board base, Jordan's layers on top.
I don't know — maybe Ms. Rivera should just decide.

a.text: ...Wait. That could actually work.
...huh. Okay. That's — actually not terrible.
So we're doing the sister's idea AND the goat idea, got it. No cap, very efficient.

b.text: ...Alright. I'll set the terms, then. Since apparently nobody here trusts each other enough to.
b.action: taps two fingers on the desk, deciding

b.text: It gets decided, but not by you. The room settles, quieter, unsatisfied.
That's one way to avoid the fight. Doesn't feel like winning, though.

lines.text: Okay so tomorrow, salamanders, non-negotiable, I already told you—
You told me. I'm still in.
You didn't play it easy today. That's — yeah. Noted.
Didn't cost me the day. Just cost me the easy version of it.
Okay, the ecosystem-slash-diorama thing? Actually ended up kind of fire. Didn't expect that.
We wouldn't have gotten there without you talking everyone down, honestly.
I just didn't want to pick a side. Turns out that was its own kind of hard.
Yeah, no cap, group grade's saved, we're all friends now, very emotional—
lines.action: finger-gun, jogging off

lines.text: Didn't blend in. Didn't blow it up either. Just — built something in between.
Casey's still salty you called his sister's poster board 'vintage,' by the way.
It was a whole aesthetic choice, no cap, don't rewrite history—
lines.action: overhearing

lines.text: ...Yeah. Vintage. Totally.
Across the lot, Jordan walks alone, headphones fully on, not looking over. Doesn't have to.
I'm in. I got exactly what I wanted at eight a.m. Funny how it doesn't feel like enough now.
Maya waved. Jordan nodded. I said 'see you' to both and meant neither, not really.
Bye, Sam! Or — Sam-I-Am? Whatever, bye!
lines.action: passing, distracted, doesn't wait for an answer

lines.text: Four schools in, and I'm still the girl in the hallway, gathering her own books, alone. Just — quieter about it now.
Didn't say the thing at lunch. Didn't say much of anything, all day, really. Tomorrow, maybe.
It's fine. Ms. Rivera sorted it. It's — whatever, it's fine.
lines.emotion: quiet, a little disappointed but not unkind

lines.text: ...I didn't know what to say back there. So I said nothing instead.
Happens. Group projects are chaos no matter what.
I tried to build a bridge and then walked off it halfway across. Next time. Maybe next time I finish it.

voiceNotes: Sam deflects with dry humor ('cool, cool, love that for me') and trails off before anything too vulnerable — her INTERNAL lines carry the emotional weight her spoken lines refuse to. Maya talks in touch-and-go bursts, assigns nicknames as a form of claiming, and goes quiet/careful right before she means something (the 'that was actually kind of cool' beat is her one unguarded moment per lunch-defend branch). Jordan hedges every sincere statement with a joke or an 'anyway,' but drops the hedging entirely when talking about their creatures — that fluency IS their vulnerability, which is why the sketchbook scene matters. Casey performs for the table (fast, run-on, checking the room after every line) and mumbles/deflates one-on-one — his cruelty about Jordan is insecurity in a jester costume, not malice, which is why he backs down easily when challenged. Ms. Rivera is economical — she never gives Jordan the yes outright, she makes them earn it, and her 'that's more than most ideas get' line is the closest she comes to warmth.

alternatives: It's twelve, thirty-one, six, I SWEAR—
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeps the fumbling authentic without over-explaining the mechanics of the locker for pacing.

alternatives: Is it too much? Like — actually too much, not nice-too-much.
Just tell me if it's weird. The real answer, not the nice one.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Alternate phrasing keeps Jordan's sideways-question pattern (asking without directly asking for reassurance) while varying delivery for pacing.

alternatives: It's not insane. It's just — actually trying, which apparently nobody else wants to do—
It's not insane, Maya. It's just more than a poster board and a Tuesday afternoon.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Offers a version where Jordan's frustration reads slightly more wounded vs. slightly more defiant, depending on tonal preference for the bridge scene.
